{{Quote|Wing riders. Riding on Thrantas. Not native to Bespin. But then few of us are.|An [[unidentified Ugnaught driver]] to [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]]|<ref name="Crossfire">''[[Boba Fett: Crossfire]]''</ref>}}
'''Thranta Riders''', also known as '''wing riders''', were [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] sapients who tamed flying [[Thranta/Legends|thrantas]] and rode them, performing aerial [[Circus/Legends|circus]]-like shows around the world of [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]]. They liked to paint or tattoo themselves with complex patterns.

During the [[New Jedi Order era]], [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] fell down from [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] while being hunted by some [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] followers. If not for [[M'kim|one]] of the Thranta Riders that saved him, he would have fallen down and been crushed from the pressure.

==Biology and appearance==
[[File:Thranta_riding.png|thumb|left|250px|Riders on the back of their mounts]]

The mysterious [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] known as the Thranta Riders were smooth-skinned [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] with four [[Limb/Legends|limbs]]: two arms ending with four-fingered hands, and two legs ending with two-toed feet. Their bodies were lanky and [[Bone/Legends|bony]], with all their muscles outlined. The riders usually went naked, showing their smooth skin on which no [[Hair/Legends|hair]] grew. Their skin was of a natural ochre yellow hue,<ref name="ISWU" /> but they used to paint it with other [[Color/Legends|colors]], including red, pale yellow, blue, pale purple and green.<ref name="TBH:AS" /> They breathed the same [[Atmosphere/Legends|air]] as [[Human/Legends|Human]] beings and were capable of shedding tears as a response to an emotional state of sorrow.<ref name="YJK:TCC" />

Many of them were dolichocephalic, with the head much longer than it was broad,<ref name="ISWU" /> while others had more Human-like cranial proportions. Though they lacked fleshy external [[Ear/Legends|ears]], the thranta-riding people had ear holes on the sides of their head, which were left exposed.<ref name="TBH:AS" /> Their faces sported two forward-facing [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], some of which were almond-shaped with yellow irises,<ref name="ISWU" /> while others were black and globulous, with no apparent iris.<ref name="TBH:AS" /> In the middle of their elongated visage, they had two tiny pug-like holes as a [[Nose/Legends|nose]], and some individuals sported a long, pointed chin.<ref name="ISWU" /> The Riders had high-pitched, singing voices that came in handy to communicate with their domesticated thrantas. At least one known individual had shining ebony [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]] that resembled polished [[gem]]stones.<ref name="YJK:TCC" />

==Society and culture==
{{Quote|The silent mysterious thranta riders often paint themselves with amazing skin patterns. Only a few lucky tourists ever catch a glimpse of their tantalizing culture.|[[Po Ruddle Lingsnot]]|<ref name="ISWU" />}}
[[File:Thranta_Riders.jpg|thumb|250px|A troupe of Thranta Riders near Cloud City around 27 BBY]]

A mysterious race, the Thranta Riders were discreet about their obscure yet fascinating culture. They usually remained silent, and, as such, only a few lucky visitors ever got a glimpse of their secrets. Even the Councilman [[Po Ruddle Lingsnot]], who when he was part of the ruling [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucrats]] of Cloud City, knew little about them. Lingsnot barely mentioned the Riders in his report describing the city, devoting a single paragraph and two brief side notes to them.<ref name="ISWU" /> One of the few details known about their intimate culture is that they referred to each other as "Air Brother."<ref name="TBH:AS" /> The Riders would often paint or tattoo themselves with complex skin patterns and whorls of colors that made them look like optical illusions.<ref name="YJK:TCC" /> Prior to going into a battle, they would apply war paints different from the regular ones.<ref name="TBH:AS" /> Although they usually wore no [[Clothing/Legends|clothes]] at all,<ref name="YJK:TCC" /> some of the Thranta Riders were noted for wearing fur [[loincloth]]s and puttees.<ref name="TBH:AS" />

Their lives revolved around the breeding and keeping of thrantas, which they trained to perform "[[sky rodeo]]" spectacles, a combination [[Circus/Legends|circus]], air show, and rodeo. In the skies around Cloud City, the Riders performed once per [[Standard month/Legends|month]], displaying their unique acrobatic feats to the public. The alien riders would leap out into the open sky, only to be caught again by their thrantas swooping down below them. Each of the Air Brothers shared a bond with his mounts, to the point where thrantas viewed their trainers as "head thranta" of their herd and guarded him from potential threats. Wranglers communicated with their thrantas through whistles or high-pitched electronic noise emulators, with different pitches or brief musical phrases being used as the signals for particular tricks.<ref name="W:RT">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20021212thranta|series=Creature Feature|text=Rodeo Thranta}}</ref>

Despite their general secrecy toward strangers, the Riders were compassionate enough to assist those in danger. When the trainee Thranta Rider [[M'kim]] saw [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]], a young [[Padawan/Legends|Jedi apprentice]], fall from a Cloud City platform, he did not hesitate before swooping down and catching him. M'kim also expressed deep sorrow by the memory of other people falling.<ref name="YJK:TCC" /> They were also willing to accept members of other species in their midst. Where this happened, the Riders would defend their new Air Brother as if he or she had always been one of them. When fighting became necessary, the Thranta Riders wielded [[Wood/Legends|wooden]], [[Club/Legends|club]]-like maces to beat off the attackers.<ref name="TBH:AS" />

==History==
{{Quote|More Thranta Riders. Armed to the teeth and painted for war. They were waiting. Someone told them I was coming!|Aurra Sing|<ref name="TBH:AS" />}}
[[File:Thrantariders.jpg|thumb|left|200px|A Thranta Rider dying at the hands of Aurra Sing]]

When the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] entrepreneur [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Ecclessis Figg/Legends|Ecclessis Figg]] commissioned the building Cloud City around [[400 BBY/Legends|400 BBY]],<ref name="GG2">''[[Galaxy Guide 2: Yavin and Bespin]]''</ref> he ordered a group of young thrantas to transplanted from their native Alderaan to Bespin's atmosphere. Figg's [[Marriage/Legends|wife]] being an [[Alderaanian/Legends|Alderaanian]], he wished to give her the thrantas as a gift to remind her of her homeworld. With the thrantas, Lord Figg also brought several Riders so they could take care of the flying creatures.<ref name="FF05">{{FactFile|5|BES 1-4|Bespin}}</ref> By [[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]],<ref name="O:MR">''[[Star Wars Omnibus: Menace Revealed]]''</ref> the Riders and their mounts had become a popular attraction thanks to their monthly performances of sky rodeo.<ref name="W:RT" /> That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], a [[Changeling/Legends|shape-shifting]] [[Shi'ido]] employed by the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] named [[Reess Kairn]] traveled to Bespin and insinuated himself into a troupe of Riders. However, the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Aurra Sing/Legends|Aurra Sing]], who had been hired to eliminate Kairn and his minions, located the changeling impostor and hopped on an [[Bespin-class airhook|airhook]] in order to kill him. When the Shi'ido cried for help to his new Air Brothers, the Thranta Riders drew their clubs and prepared to fight Sing. One of them successfully threw the bounty hunter off her vehicle, but another was killed when Aurra Sing dropped down on his thranta to stop her fall. In spite of their efforts, the Riders could not stop the bounty hunter, and the Shi'ido was murdered.<ref name="TBH:AS" />

Over five decades later, in [[24 ABY/Legends|24 ABY]], the trainee Rider M'kim saved the life of a young Human by catching him out of the sky when he fell off Cloud City. That Human was none other than Jacen Solo, an apprentice to [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], who was chased by [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] agents and plummeted into the atmosphere while trying to escape them. Solo and M'kim then became friends, and the latter provided vital information [[Czethros]]—a [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] who was one of the more powerful lieutenants in the Black Sun at the time.<ref name="YJK:TCC" />

==Behind the scenes==
As part of the concept art designed by Ralph McQuarrie but ultimately not used for [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']], the Thranta Riders were known as "What-Nots."
